verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family tyrosine kinase, N-myristoylated and palmitoylated, anchoring it to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane at baseline. However, ecto-LYN has been documented on the surface of tumor cells (notably in certain leukemias/solid tumors) as part of the cancer ecto-kinase phenomenon, where inner-leaflet kinases are displayed on the outer leaflet of transformed cells. This qualifies as cell-state-induced surface exposure.
